title: Denied Because You Already Have a Degree
desc: VR&E says your current degrees or credentials already qualify you for work, so additional training is unnecessary.
problemRouterLabel: They denied me because I already have a degree

steps.title: They denied me because I already have a degree

fields.name: existingCredentials
fields.label: What degrees or certifications do you already have?
fields.placeholder: e.g. B.S. in Nursing and MBA

fields.name: currentCredentialJobs
fields.label: What jobs do those credentials usually qualify you for?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Bedside nursing or patient-facing management roles

fields.name: unsuitableWhy
fields.label: Why are those jobs unsuitable because of service-connected disabilities?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Migraines and PTSD make patient-facing work unsafe

fields.name: targetOccupation
fields.label: What specific target occupation are you now requesting?

fields.name: requiredCredential
fields.label: What degree, license, or credential is normally required for that occupation?

fields.name: vaRationale
fields.label: Did VA explain why your current credentials already lead to suitable employment?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Said I could work anywhere with my MBA

intakeQuestions: What degrees or certifications do you already have?
What jobs do those credentials qualify you for?
Why are those jobs unsuitable because of service-connected disabilities?
Are you unemployed, underemployed, or working in a job that aggravates your conditions?
What specific target occupation are you requesting?
What degree, license, or credential is normally required for that occupation?
likelyVaErrors: The office may be conflating any employment with suitable employment.
The file may not distinguish between being able to get a job and being able to obtain and maintain suitable work without worsening disabilities.
recordsToRequest: Evidence that current jobs aggravate service-connected conditions
Labor-market evidence for the requested occupation
Any VA rationale claiming current credentials are already marketable
errors: Denials based on existing degrees often skip the separate question of whether those credentials lead to suitable, sustainable employment.
The record may ignore how the veteran’s disabilities limit the jobs those current credentials usually support.

evidenceChecklist.text: Evidence current or qualifying jobs aggravate disabilities or are otherwise unsuitable
Evidence the requested occupation needs the new degree or credential
Evidence current credentials are not producing suitable employment outcomes
